title.zh: 十日元硬币上的降灵问答
title.en: Table-turning reborn as a ten-yen seance

summary.zh: 纸上写五十音与鸟居，众人手指按住十日元硬币，念「狐狗狸大人请降临」，硬币便自行移动作答。源自1884年传入的西洋转桌术，1970年代在校园大流行。
summary.en: Fingers on a ten-yen coin over a paper of kana and a torii gate, children chant 'Kokkuri-san, please come,' and the coin moves to spell answers. Western table-turning arrived in 1884; the school craze peaked in the 1970s.

description.zh:
现代狐狗狸的做法在日本校园里代代相传：一张纸，上方画鸟居，写「是」「否」，下面排开五十音图与数字，参加者各伸一根手指按住十日元硬币，齐声念「狐狗狸大人、狐狗狸大人，请您降临」。硬币开始滑动，在字符间拼出回答——考试的题目、喜欢的人的名字、死期。规则里堆满禁忌：中途不能松手，结束必须恭送（「请您回去」），用过的纸要撕碎，硬币要在当天花掉；违反的下场是被狐灵附身。1970年代这套游戏在全国中小学大流行，因集体癔症事件频发，许多学校明令禁止——禁令本身又成了它魅力的一部分。

它的出身却是舶来品。1884年（明治17年），西洋帆船的船员把当时欧美灵媒热中的转桌术（table-turning）传到伊豆半岛，玩法是三根竹子架起饭桶盖，众人按手其上待其倾动。这套「不可思议」迅速风靡全国，因倾动之态被称为「こっくり」（点头貌），再附会上汉字「狐狗狸」——狐、狗、狸三兽之灵。哲学家井上圆了在《妖怪玄谈》（1887年）里对其做了逐条解剖，判定为参加者无意识的肌肉运动（不觉筋动），这是日本近代「妖怪学」的开山案例之一。

从明治的饭桶盖到昭和的十日元硬币，狐狗狸完成了从沙龙降灵到校园仪式的驯化，与西方的通灵板（Ouija board）是同源异形的兄弟。恐怖漫画与电影里，它至今是「不该打开的门」的标准开场。
description.en:
The modern form is handed down through Japanese schoolyards: a sheet of paper with a torii gate drawn at the top, 'yes' and 'no,' and the fifty kana with numerals below; each player rests one finger on a ten-yen coin and all chant, 'Kokkuri-san, Kokkuri-san, please come.' The coin begins to slide, spelling out answers, exam questions, a crush's name, the date of one's death. The rules bristle with taboos: never lift your finger midway, always send the spirit off with 'please return home,' tear up the paper, spend the coin that day. Violators are possessed by the fox spirit. The game swept elementary and middle schools in the 1970s, trailing episodes of mass hysteria that led many schools to ban it, and the bans became part of the allure.

Its pedigree is imported. In 1884 sailors from a Western ship introduced table-turning, then the rage of European and American spiritualism, at the Izu peninsula; the Japanese version balanced a rice-tub lid on three bamboo stalks and read the tilting as speech. The craze crossed the country under the name kokkuri, 'the nodding,' soon dressed in the characters for fox, dog, and tanuki. The philosopher Inoue Enryo dissected it in his Yokai Gendan (1887), diagnosing unconscious muscular movement, a founding case of Meiji 'monsterology' and its campaign of disenchantment.

From the Meiji rice-tub lid to the Showa ten-yen coin, Kokkuri-san was domesticated from parlor seance into playground rite, a sibling of the Western Ouija board by parallel descent. In horror manga and film it remains the standard first scene of a door that should have stayed shut.

value.en: 1884, via Western table-turning at the Izu peninsula

value.zh: 鸟居五十音纸与十日元硬币
value.en: A kana sheet with a torii and a ten-yen coin

value.zh: 井上圆了归因于「不觉筋动」（1887年）

sources.zh: 朝里树《日本现代怪异事典》（2018年）
sources.en: Asazato Itsuki, Nihon Gendai Kaii Jiten (2018)

label.zh: 伊豆下田（转桌术登陆地）
